sous_titres.text: It is time for the money.
I'm American. What do you expect?
Rubles don't exactly go a long way these days.
Okay? After that, I can't promise anything.
So much for not leaving a trace.
although we've never met, I consider you all family.
If you're getting this email, you, like me,
And right now you need to know the following.
I don't know how many there are of us.
We all train to be a part of a program called the Internship.
And we were left to rot when the program went down in flames
We were taken from our mothers and fathers as children,
So guys, the... the tusks on this guy,
they actually grow from the bottom instead of the top
Some of us were forced to see in the dark,
until we could see things that others could not.
Others of us were pushed past our limits
and be able to fight even if we were shot or stabbed.
taught to survive in freezing cold or extreme heat,
or go without oxygen for up to 10 minutes.
I am reaching out over a matter of life or death.
for us to meet and determine our next move.
If you choose not to come here,
then wherever you are, get the fuck out.
Who are you? I'm with Catalyst.
but the reason we don't have the Internship
Are you being serious? I am always serious.
Our four interns, they lost their minds
and killed themselves and killed their handlers.
We had to eradicate an entire crop of trainees.
Well, when the program is destroyed,
including the handwritten notes by Byrne himself.
Well, did she know where we were?
we could not get it right.
And where we felt the Americans succeeded.
the program has been activated again.
If we cannot have our own program,
that the Americans don't have it either.
And how do I do that? Huh?
The intern that did this is traveling to New York.
New York. Are you sure? Oh. Quite.
And we have an agent there?
Well, we have our sources, Vlad, and you have your orders.
You wanted to see me, sir?
I certainly do. Come on in.
You know Dick Jones, deputy Director of Operations.
I'm about to put you right in the middle of one.
Tell me, what have you heard about the Internship?
It was started by former director of operations,
and the program was used to weaponize children for the CIA
until Byrne went rogue and it was abandoned.
The Internship ended with Byrne's death.
Somehow the Russians found out about it
and eventually it was abandoned as well.
Intern by the name of Renee James,
code name Catalyst, showed up at FSB headquarters in Moscow.
Catalyst walked out with sensitive information,
including leads to the location of the remaining interns.
list of dark web email addresses.
We tried contacting the interns previously.
We never received a single reply.
The interns went to dark after Byrne's death.
And they ignored our request to come in from the cold.
Nobody's heard from them in all of these years until now.
They think she might be trying to build a substitute family
for the one she never had.
You mean the one that was stolen from her?
Nobody likes what was done to these children.
But we can't have them running around without any control.
We have to bring these assets back in house
or shut them down for good.
Can I ask why you want me for this assignment?
I mean, surely there are better people suited for this job.
There may be others with more experience,
but you are uniquely qualified for this job, agent.
But there are two things you need to know.
we believe there's a Russian mole,
Second, Renee James. Code name, Catalyst...
Come with me if you want to live.
I always wanted to say that. Get in.
Wha-- Wha-- What brings you around here?
I'm hungry. And I heard you make a mean burger.
Something is not right about this picture.
Come on. You know my story. I'm fired.
No, "Thank you for your service, Nelson."
That's what happens when you knock your boss on his ass.
Well, that's because Dick Jones's an asshole
Oh, maybe. But did you really picture yourself
flipping burgers on a food truck?
You ever think you could have handled it differently?
And I don't remember how much of an asshole Dick Jones is
and know that I'd do it again in a heartbeat.
It's a good burger. I know.
I need someone I can trust.
I'm not really sure I can trust anybody in this world anymore.
And there's something I didn't tell you.
When I was 17, way before you and me,
and I wasn't ready to be a mom, so I gave her up for adoption.
And I just found out who my daughter is.
And I need your help to find her before the Russians do.
They didn't tell me you were coming.
They thought I was finished with the KGB.
You are never finished with this kind of obligation, Father.
You should know this by now.
And we are FSB, not KGB.
Today. This is extremely short notice.
There is a cafe down the street.
We will convene with a man named Uri.
Now give me your blessing, father.
So we look like good people.
So, what do you know about the Internship?
It was an agency program that went off the rails years ago.
Uh... ...so your daughter is somehow involved?
It would seem so, and I have no idea how.
I didn't even know where she was until 24 hours ago.
Look, I was a kid when I had my kid
and my parents disowned me and...
I didn't know what to do.
I gave her up for adoption and joined the army
and they put me through school and then I ended up in the CIA.
your daughter ended up in the Internship.
Fate is cruel, it would seem.
And now the CIA wants her, dead or alive, they don't care.
And the Russians want her, just dead.
And I really need you to help me find her
Why would the Russians want her dead?
It makes sense. Think about it.
I mean, the director said the Russians tried to start up
their own version of the program
and it went up in flames and now they're worried
So, if the Russians are gonna come here and go after her...
they're gonna send somebody that's good.
It's an old friend of mine.
I hope he's a good one.
We have had our disagreements over the years.
So, the last time that I saw him...
he was trying to kill me.
Uri was wondering if you could come back... tomorrow?
You better go. He is very busy today.
She turned for help to a former CIA operative, Nelson Beatty.
I know him. We have, uh...
I know you do. You let him live.
Because he does not die easily.
You just need the right motivation.
I'm sending you with the coordinates where the intern,
and I am the only one who needs to know.
Now, get on with your work.
And you would know that, right? Because you're what, 11?
I don't know. I think it was endearing.
I'm sorry, I didn't know this was gonna be the children's hour.
And if you get shot, stabbed,
or in any way mutilated or spindled,
I'm gonna be the best friend you have, Grumpy.
Thank you for being here, really.
If you haven't already met her, Apothecary, meet everyone.
Why'd you bring a knife to a gun fight, girl?
I have no problem with guns,
but sometime, a knife has an advantage.
Would you like to see that again?
No one here was ever a kid.
Remember what they put us all through?
Forced us to fight, trained us to kill.
It made us what we are today.
So, you know, we can choose to hide
and mope and pretend we're something that we're not,
or we can embrace what we've become
and show the world what we're capable of.
You guys are like the closest to a family I'm ever gonna have,
and personally, I'm really tired of hiding.
You've really been practicing that speech, haven't you?
you guys kind of know a little bit about me
from my email, so, why don't we
just take this time to go around the room,
let's just keep it to the greatest hits.
My name's Julie. Code name, Apothecary.
I grew up mostly in New York.
I don't know how old I was when I first entered the Internship.
Before it went to hell, I was working
with the Israeli embassy, photographing secret documents,
and on their behalf, I took out a number of high-level insurgents
using an untraceable herb that I put in their tea.
I think I'm French. I don't know for sure.
working for a French cabinet member
until I was ordered to kill him.
Once upon a time I was known as Elliot,
but I've been Rubicon for a long while now.
Finished most of my training in Northern Italy,
After that, I was working with a tech billionaire in the UK.
Taught me a hell of a lot.
Well, wasn't until he committed suicide.
Well, let's just say I didn't kill him.
I'm just good at hurting people.
Last but not least, our, uh,
Uh, I've got the basic intern skills,
What I'm really good at is money.
Making it, understanding how it works.
And they put me with a Russian attaché
working in Philadelphia who loved to gamble.
I shut 'em out to play the stock market.
Uh, my handler said I look Russian.
You guys think I look Russian? Not in those slippers.
Hey big man. Let's see a menu.
Am I... sitting in the wrong position?
It kind of looks open to me.
I'm actually here to see Vlad.
And you know who I'm talking about.
Now go back to where you came from.
Now, tell Vlad to call me, all right?
I can guarantee you he's gonna wanna talk to me.
Tonight is a good night for you to curl up,
with a good book next to fireplace.
Perhaps you should make pretend to turn on the stove.
Open the door and leave the keys. Get out.
Looks like Ms. Dalton has brought Nelson Jameson in
to help her. Do you have a problem with that?
The son of a bitch hit me. Well, there are those who might say you had it coming.
I know you two don't like each other.
I don't even remember breathing last night.
Thought everybody could use some sleep.
It was valerian root, guys, not sleeping pills.
All right. Um, listen, the other day I stole this list
from the FSB headquarters in Moscow.
It's a list of all the interns.
You wouldn't believe what I went through to get it.
I reached out to everybody else on that list. You guys were the only ones who showed up,
so we might be all that's left of the Internship.
That's what we're trying to figure out.
could be a routing number for a bank.
And how do you know that?
Like I said, good with money.
Plus, I know seven different ways to kill with my thumbs.
So, the dollar amount, they could be going
to some sort of slush fund or something?
Maybe? It's not that much money.
In what world is that not a lot of money?
Yeah, man, that's more than a million dollars.
One million, two hundred and seventy-seven thousand,
to be exact. But he's right, though.
It's actually not a lot of money,
and have access to unlimited funds.
Can you check the routing number, please?
It's for United First Bank in New York City.
Who do you think they are?
I better put on some shoes.
Mine's the biggest. Of course it is.
The bigger the gun, the smaller the--
We have to be out front in two minutes. Meet me out there.
So, we're friends now. Are we?
The last time I saw you, you had a knife on my throat
and you were trying to kill me. Remember?
You were trying to kill me.
- Me neither. - I thought you were out.
Somebody asked me to do them a favor,
Do what you gotta do, friend.
I put our van at the end of the trail,
so if any of us get separated, that's where we're meeting up.
They're coming at us from the east and the west,
This right here, this paint, uh,
marks the trail that's very difficult to follow.
So, you're gonna take the lead.
How come he gets to take the lead?
Because I need you with me in the back to take up the rear.
We head out to the snow.
Okay. Let's get this van cleared.
Keep pressure on that, big boy.
Ah, karma. It's a funny thing.
How did the Americans find us?
You couldn't leave well enough alone,
Yeah, the whole place is a mess.
Somehow the Russians got to the interns before we did.
Back channel chatter also confirms that.
And Catalyst, and the others don't know they're compromised.
They're walking right into a shit show.
you find the interns and you find the mole.
Unless the Russians find them first.
The only thing I've got for her is an address on the dark web.
I'm sending it to you now.
Just enough Demerol to take the edge off.
I told you that would happen.
At least that's what they're calling themselves.
Hmm. Any idea who it could be?
No, but there was someone on the ridge backing us up.
Do you think he's a friend?
I don't know what I think.
- How can we trust you? - Well,
do you really have any other options?
Great. Well, one point for you.
But, um, I just don't get why,
if you got kicked out, why did they send you?
Why? Were you cloned in a lab?
- Every living being has a mother. - Yeah. No.
but whoever my mother was, I never met her.
I was raised by the Internship.
Your mother was 17 when she got pregnant,
She had no home, no money.
She gave you up for adoption and she joined the military.
She was guaranteed somewhere to sleep,
She discovered she was quite good at it.
So, she was recruited to the CIA.
Now, her mission is to bring you in.
And she reached out to you why?
Okay, yeah. Okay. The two of you.
What you should get is the CIA once you're brought in alive.
The Russians want to kill you as you've just witnessed.
I put a tracker on one of the Russians.
And to answer your next question,
I don't know how they found you.
Is there a mole in my team?
Now the Russian that is hunting you,
He would've found you on his own.
So, uh, what do you think I should do?
I propose you're gonna meet your mother.
If you're uncomfortable, then you walk away.
But if you're lying to me, you're a dead man.
Let's try and avoid that, shall we?
Great. Park your car. We're taking ours.
So, you're planning to rob a bank?
So, that's the list I retrieved
from the FSB headquarters in Moscow.
And that is a routing number for the First Union Bank.
We think they're the coordinates to the New York City branch.
We were hoping that you could tell us that, actually.
Your guess is as good as mine.
How am I supposed to know you are who you say you are.
But whether I'm your mom or not, I still gotta keep you safe.
you need to keep the world safe from us.
You think I don't know that.
I'm deputy director of operations, CIA.
I have orders to bring the assets in.
Get your people in line, Catalyst. We're here to help you.
Come on. Come on. Come on. Go, go, go, go. Come on.
Get in the car! Get in!
Do not let them get away.
Watch out, watch out! Coming through!
What the bloody hell was that?
You die before you touch that.
Why are you hunting the interns?
how'd you know where we were gonna meet?
A sniper shot came from nearby.
Was the shooter working with Dick Jones?
He seemed as surprised as everyone else.
And yet he was trying to kill the interns?
I'm coming to your location. Stay put.
Someone set us up. Was it you?
I'm not gonna dignify that with an answer, agent.
I can make this real hard on you, Vlad.
you have no idea what hard is.
that you place so much value upon.
- Let me see your wound. - Get off! I'm fine.
We need to get to the city and we need to get to the bank.
But there's something we ought to deal with first.
That sniper shot came from a Russian agent.
Twice now, he's found us. Twice.
Once. Yeah, sure. Maybe the guy's good, but twice?
Everyone, get out of the van.
Could be any one of you.
Rubicon, I think it's really convenient
your car explodes right after you get out.
Of all this lot, you're accusing me?
Have you fallen out of your tree or something?
Apothecary, you knocked everyone out with your tea.
Who were you calling that night?
What are you talking about? I didn't call anyone.
Dagger. Why are you always texting?
Who are you texting? I'm not texting.
Analyst, what about your Russian connection?
They put me with a Russian attaché working in Philadelphia
That is bullshit.  It wasn't my choice where they place me--
And Caliber... you seemed real interested in what was in my bag.
I thought it was mine. They are both identical.
How could you even-- Identical? They're completely different.
How could you even accuse me...
You know what... I don't even...
What the hell does drug dealing have to do with being a mole?
Take your phones out, unlock them and give them to me.
- It's bloody ridiculous. - Come on, give 'em here.
Man, how you gonna lock me up with him, man?
"Meeting at an Airbnb in upstate New York."
And remember, I'm the one who's seen your file.
The attaché I was assigned took me to Russia.
So how long you been working for the Russians?
I'm not. The guy was a rat.
I never even heard that guy's name till he said it.
Stop lying! I'm telling the truth.
What do the Russians have planned?
- Maybe it was, uh-- - It was you.
I knew it. You are the traitor. You're the mole!
Guy's a fucking rat! Oh my...
Yeah. We needed intel from him. Renee, he ran.
He was still one of us.
You know what? Now, he's not.
Well, let's get rid of him. Great.
Gonna take a family trip to the bank.
- This should be it. - Check it out.
Excuse me. Um, did this used to be a bank?
I don't know about any banks here, dude.
This place has been here for a couple of years.
I've been working here since it started.
I think it was a bar before this.
Hasn't been a bank for several years.
I'll take a small black coffee.
You mean a short? I mean a small.
Thank you. Um, does the phrase
I dunno maybe a store on the street or something?
A store? Not that I can remember.
Do you mean the old phone exchange?
Although I don't know what a phone exchange is.
God, am I really that old?
Once upon a time, before area codes,
to represent the first two numbers on the dial.
BT was an eight, and the R would've been seven.
So, Trafalgar nine meant the prefix was 8, 7, 9.
But we didn't use that exchange in this neighborhood.
Do you know where they did? Not sure.
There was another branch of the bank uptown, maybe that's it.
Yeah. This is where the other branch of the bank was located.
Yeah, except it looks like it's been closed for years.
Uh, ten years, to be exact.
Ten years and nobody ain't been there since.
I'm not so sure about that.
It's a schematic drawing of the city block.
Yeah. That's where the travel agency was located.
And you found all that online. Everything's online, bro.
What planet have you been living on?
There is like a secret basement where they're doing something terrible.
Well, not according to the schematic,
...this building pays a massive electricity bill every month.
Then we're in the right place.
whatever's in there is probably what we've been looking for.
So, I don't know about y'all, but I'm going.
Well it's like you said, "We're interns."
What's happening? All right, let's fix it.
I don't think I'll ever get bored of doing that.
According to this old architect's drawing,
there should be a staircase right...
They should really upgrade that security system.
There's only one place they can go.
Just let 'em find what they're looking for.
They're not trying to keep anybody out.
Yeah. No. It's designed to keep people in.
I didn't know that that was part of your training back then.
My handler thought it'd come in handy one day.
We're not CIA and neither are you.
The company doesn't know what you're doing now, does it?
But I am making this country a safer place.
You see, there's always that one person
throughout history and time who sees things that others don't.
We created a new breed of intern.
It's infinitely better than the old program.
Unlike you lot, this batch is next generation
All of your strengths and none of your... flaws.
but we've taken his idea to the next level
and they will protect this country.
No, no. I'm not just handing over the assets.
No, they're not. You of all people should know that.
Were you just gonna shoot me now?
Let's finish what we started. Huh?
Let's go. You're so fucking predicta--
You would just love to kill me, wouldn't you?
I'd rather you rot in prison.
Yeah, that's what I'm saying. None of these kids exist.
Agent Dalton, I'm putting you in charge to clean this mess up.
and see to it that our program is kept under our supervision.
Then we'll see where we go from here. Right?
I want Nelson to work with me, sir.
I want her by my side every step of the way
No one will understand them better than she will.
If I'm gonna agree to this, I have some demands of my own.
Firstly, they're gonna live normal lives.
They're gonna have birthday parties and watch TV.
Not get tortured. No, no, no, no, no torture.
And I have no problem with birthday parties or TV.
In fact, let 'em go ahead on dates.
Well, we can save the dates for when they're teenagers, but yeah.
she reports into you and you report to me.
Are we good on that? Yes, sir. Thank you, sir.
Okay, fellas, let's get these bodies outta here.
look, I know this isn't gonna happen overnight.
We got a lot of years to make up for, right?
But I just want you to be patient and
And everything we do with these kids, we do together
Hmm. What do we do next?
I mean, I'm sure there's a position for everybody.
I mean, after all, we're interns.
Hands here. Everybody, interns on three.
It could be a cute moment, though.
I knew we could rely on you.
Oh, some things are a long time in the making,
Let me tell you that your country is proud of you.
I wish I could spend longer, but we don't have time.
I just want to say now,
I wish you much success in your new position.
It's best that you speak English.
I promise you, we will take care of your daughter,
Speak English. Take her to the Internship.
They're waiting for her. Yes, sir.
You will learn to feel no pain.
Never let them see you feel pain.
Don't show them what you're thinking or feeling.
Remember everything I told you. You are strong.
And I promise that I will always find a way to see you.
